body:
There's a few issues with `mean!`: 

1. Broadcasting over `Field` directly is slow. So this PR changes flavors of `Statistics.mean` to use broadcasting over the underlying data rather than `Field` as an optimization.

2. `conditional_length` / `conditional_sum` is slow. Not surprising because we have to sum up all the active cells (in general). This PR tries to speed it up for non-immersed grids (but this optimization may not get touched ultimately because optimization 3 below)

3. We have several shortcuts for non-immersed grids, but we were missing one for `condition_operand(::typeof(Base.identity), ...)` so this PR adds that.

The objective is to make these two more similar:

```julia
using Oceananigans
using Statistics
using BenchmarkTools

grid = RectilinearGrid(size=(8, 8, 8), extent=(1, 1, 1), halo=(1, 1, 1))
u = XFaceField(grid)
U = Field{Nothing, Nothing, Nothing}(grid)
Ui = zeros(1, 1, 1)

@benchmark mean!(U, u)
@benchmark mean!(Ui, interior(u))
```
title: Collection of optimizations for `mean!` and `_mean`
